header.garden.you: You must place a Garden card
header.objective.you: Validate an objective or <pass>end your turn</pass>
header.objective.pass: You cannot validate an objective: <pass>end your turn</pass>
header.reveal-card: A new Garden card is revealed
board.objectives: At the top of the board are the <objective>Royal Objectives</objective>.
board.enhancements: In the middle are the 3 <enhancement>enhancement tiles</enhancement> available for purchase, for 2, 3 or 4 <gold>gold coins</gold>.
board.cards: At the bottom are the 12 <card>Garden cards</card> available.
board.cards.rules: To take a Garden card, you need an unoccupied space on the <mini>mini-board</mini> for your <gardener>gardener</gardener> in the corresponding column, and you need to pay between 0 and 3 <tool>tools</tool>, depending on which row the card is in.
mini-board.players: Under each column, there are as many spaces as the number of players in the game.
card.deck: There are {cards} cards left in the deck.
card.garden.you: This card is on the {level, plural, =1{1st} =2{2nd} other{#rd}} level of your garden.
card.garden.player: This card is on the {level, plural, =1{1st} =2{2nd} other{#rd}} level of {player}’s garden.
card.enhancement: <enhancement>An enhancement</enhancement> is placed on this card.
card.free: This card is available for free.
card.irrigated: <irrigated/>: The <card>Irrigation card</card> you were given at the start of the game shows you where you need to place your irrigated Garden cards to score those victory points at the end of the game. You may place irrigated cards elsewhere in your garden, but they will not score as part of your Irrigation layout.
card.flowers: <flowers/>: Your goal here is to create the largest bloom of flowers you can. At the <score>end of the game</score>, you only score points for your largest connected group of flowers in each color.
card.animal.1: <animal/>: lions like to lounge on the first level. {max} points on the first level, {min} points otherwise.
card.animal.2: <animal/>: monkeys prefer to play on the second level. {max} points on the second level, {min} points otherwise.
card.animal.3: <animal/>: peacocks like the calm on the third level. {max} points on the third level, {min} points otherwise.
card.tree: <tree/>: your goal here is to group trees of the same species in your garden. A single tree scores the first value, two adjacent trees of the same species score the second value, and so on. When you reach the maximum group size, you start a new group.
card.tree.11: Socotra dragon tree: Maximum 2 trees for a total of 7 points.
card.tree.12: Cedar of Lebanon: Maximum 3 trees for a total of 9 points.
card.tree.13: Date palm: Maximum 4 trees for a total of 18 points.
visitor.21: 1 victory point for each blue flower.
visitor.22: 1 victory point for each yellow flower.
visitor.23: 1 victory point for each red flower.
visitor.28: 1 victory point for each Crown bonus.
visitor.29: 1 victory point for each Gold coins bonus.
visitor.30: 1 victory point for each Tools bonus.
visitor.31: 2 victory points for each completed Royal Objective.
visitor.32: 2 victory points for each enhancement purchased.
visitor.33: 2 victory points for each trio of flowers: one blue, one red and one yellow.
visitor.34: 3 victory points for each trio: a tree, a visitor and an animal.
card.empty: Empty card: during the game, you can purchase enhancements. These improve your Empty cards, to help you build the best garden possible.
card.crown: <crown/>: immediately take <marker>the first-player marker</marker>. While you hold it, you will be the first player in the next round and benefit from a reduction on all <objective>Royal Objectives</objective>.
card.gold: <icon/>: when you place this bonus, you immediately gain the same number of <gold>gold coins</gold> as the level on which you place it.
card.tools: <icon/>: when you place this bonus, you immediately gain the same number of <tool>tools</tool> as the level on which you place it.
enhancement.rule: You can buy one enhancement per turn. Enhancements are always placed on an Empty Garden card.
objective.5: 3 Gold coins bonuses on the same level.
objective.6: 3 Tools bonuses on the same level.
objective.7: 3 Crown bonuses on the same level.
objective.8: 4 blue flowers on the same level.
objective.9: 4 yellow flowers on the same level.
objective.10: 4 red flowers on the same level.
objective.complete: At the end of your turn, you can complete <bold>a single Royal Objective</bold>.
objective.discount: If you hold <marker>the first-player marker</marker>, you can complete a <objective>Royal Objective</objective> using 1 fewer items.
objective.mark: When you complete a <objective>Royal Objective</objective>, place one of your <marker>Objective markers</marker> on one of the free spaces for that objective.
objective.bonus: If there is a Tool <tool/> or Gold <gold/> icon on that space, you immediately gain that bonus.
irrigation.you: You received this irrigation card at the start of the game.
irrigation.player: {player} received this irrigation card at the start of the game.
gardener.rule: To take a garden card, you must place your Gardener on an available space at the bottom of the corresponding column on the board.
gardener.round: The round ends when all players have placed all their Gardeners. Take back all your Gardeners and begin the next round.
first-player.round: The player in possession of <bold>the first-player marker</bold> when a new round begins plays first.
gold.use: Gold coins can be used to buy <enhancement>enhancements</enhancement>.
tool.use: Tools are used to acquire Garden cards that are not at the bottom of their column.
scorepad.score: Score your victory points as follows:
scorepad.irrigation: Irrigation <irrigation/>: Count the number of irrigated Garden cards in the places specified on <card>your Irrigation card</card>. Score the corresponding number of victory points.
scorepad.blooms: Blooms <blue/> / <red/> / <yellow/>: Only your largest connected group in each flower color (red, yellow and blue) scores. Gain 1 point per flower in those groups.
scorepad.trees: Trees <tree/>: Score the number of points shown for your groups of connected trees of the same species.
scorepad.animals: Animals <animal/>: Score the number of points for the level on which your animal is placed.
scorepad.visitors: Visitors <visitor/>: Score the points shown if the features your visitor wants to see are in your garden.
scorepad.objectives: Royal Objectives <icon/>: Score the same number of points as <objective>Royal Objectives</objective> you have completed.
scorepad.winner: The winner is the player with the most victory points.
scorepad.tie: If there is a tie, the winner is the player with the most tools and gold coins. If there is still a tie, the tied players both win.
comment.1: Your garden is a total wasteland! Even Nebuchadnezzar would hesitate to hang a pot of basil in it.
comment.2: A few timid shoots... But we're a long way from the Hanging Gardens: put some fertilizer on it!
comment.3: Well done! Your plot is starting to look like a corner of the Hanging Gardens of Babylon.
comment.4: A botanical masterpiece worthy of Queen Amytis herself! The gardening gods salute you.
tuto.welcome: Welcome to the tutorial for <bold>Hanging Gardens</bold>!
tuto.goal:
You play a landscape gardener commissioned by the Babylonian king Nebuchadnezzar II to design the hanging gardens for Queen Amytis.
Your goal is to create the most beautiful garden.
tuto.cards: Here are the <bold>Garden cards</bold>. There are always 12 Garden cards available, in 3 columns on the board.
tuto.turn:
On your turn, choose a Garden card and place it in your personal garden.
Place this card in your garden.
tuto.gardener:
Here are your <bold>Gardeners</bold>.
When you take a card, you place one of these Gardeners at the bottom of the corresponding column.
tuto.gardener.space: If there is no longer a free space for a Gardener at the bottom of a column, players can no longer take Garden cards from that column.
tuto.opponent.1:
It’s up to your opponent.
Players take it in turns to place their 3 Gardener counters.
tuto.gardener.blocked: It’s up to you. The cards in this column are no longer available, as there is no longer a free space for a Gardener counter at the bottom of the column.
tuto.tools:
This is your stock of <bold>Tools</bold>.
<italic>Players start with 4 tools (5 tools for the last player).</italic>
tuto.tools.pay: Take this card and place it in your garden. It will cost you 1 Tool.
tuto.vp:
The cards you place in your garden will earn you victory points at the end of the game.
For example, this lion will earn you 5 victory points, as it is placed on the first level of your garden.
<bold>At any time, you can click on a Garden card to see its effects.</bold>
tuto.opponent.2: Your opponent has played his second card, and it’s your turn again.
tuto.garden: Here’s your <bold>Irrigation card</bold>. It represents the final shape of your garden (5 cards on the first level, 4 on the second and 3 on the top level).
tuto.irrigation: Your Irrigation card also tells you how to obtain victory points at the end of the game by judiciously placing your irrigated cards: <irrigated/>.
tuto.floor2: Take this card and place it on the second level of your garden.
tuto.bonus: This card’s bonus allows you to gain as many Tools as the level on which it is placed: you therefore gain 2 Tools.
tuto.enhance.cost: Gold is used to buy <bold>Enhancement tiles</bold>. You can buy a maximum of one enhancement per turn, and it costs 2, 3 or 4 gold coins.
tuto.enhance.place:
Enhancements are placed on empty cards, like this one (without enhancements).
Enhancements allow you to score more points by improving your empty cards.
tuto.enhance.buy: Buy this enhancement and place it on this empty card.
tuto.objective: At the end of your turn you also have the option of <bold>validating a Royal Objective</bold>.
tuto.objective.flowers:
To complete an objective, you must have the requested items <bold>on the same level</bold>.
For example, this one requires 4 red flowers on the same level.
tuto.objective.count: You have 3 red flowers on the first level of your garden.
tuto.objective.reduce:
However, you are also in possession of the <bold>first-player marker</bold>.
As long as it’s in your possession, you can complete objectives with <bold>one fewer item</bold>.
tuto.objective.complete: Validate the objective by clicking on it.
tuto.opponent.3: Your opponent will now play his last gardener.
tuto.opponent.crown:
The card he has just taken has a crown bonus: <crown/>.
This bonus allows you to take the first-player marker.
tuto.round: It’s the end of the first round: all players take back their Gardeners.
tuto.end-game: The game continues in this way for 4 rounds, at the end of which each player will have a complete garden, made up of 12 cards.
tuto.score:
At the end of the game, this scorepad is used to tally each player’s victory points.
The player with the most points wins.
<bold>Click on the scorepad to find out all the ways to score points: irrigation, garden and objectives.</bold>
tuto.complete: Now it’s your turn to play, and good luck!
